{{Short description|None}}
{{EngvarB|date=November 2023}}

{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2020}}

This is a '''list of the men's national association football teams''' in the world.  The [[FIFA|Fédération Internationale de Football Association]] (FIFA) is the world's most important governing body for [[association football]]. A majority of national [[association football]] teams in the world are members of FIFA or one of its affiliated [[FIFA confederation|continental confederations]]. A majority of them also represent [[sovereign states]] with wide international recognition, with 188 of 195 [[United Nations]] (UN) member and [[United Nations General Assembly observers#Present non-member observers|observer states]] holding membership of FIFA. A notable exception is the [[United Kingdom]], which is not a member of FIFA in its own right but is represented in the governing body by the teams of its four [[constituent countries of the United Kingdom|constituent countries]]. Other UN members and one observer state have either held membership in a confederation in the past and subsequently lost it, or have never belonged to either FIFA or one of its recognised confederations.

Membership of FIFA and its confederations also includes national teams representing a few [[state with limited recognition|states with limited international recognition]], one [[associated state]] with no UN membership, a significant number of [[dependent territories]], and a limited number of [[list of autonomous areas by country|autonomous areas]]. A majority of states with limited recognition, however, have no membership of FIFA or any of its confederations.

This list divides national teams into three main groups:

* Teams that are either members of FIFA (211 teams), or have membership in a FIFA-affiliated continental confederation without being members of FIFA itself (11 teams).
* Teams that are not members of FIFA or any continental federation, but which represent UN member and observer states. This group includes five United Nations member states and one observer state (6 teams).
* Teams representing states with limited international recognition and no membership of FIFA or a confederation (6 teams), plus one representing an associated state with no membership of FIFA or a confederation.

This list excludes other teams described by their supporters as 'national', which represent specific [[ethnic group]]s, cultural/historical regions, [[list of active separatist movements|separatist or autonomist movements]], speakers of specific languages, and [[micronation]]s. These teams have historically participated in matches and tournaments [[Non-FIFA football|outside FIFA's recognition]], though some of them have sporadically or regularly played in friendly matches and tournaments against FIFA members.

Some national teams with FIFA membership have disappeared due to belonging to a sovereign state or dependent territory which split into two or more states or territories (examples include the [[Soviet Union national football team|Soviet Union]], [[Yugoslavia national football team|Yugoslavia]], or the [[Netherlands Antilles national football team|Netherlands Antilles]]), or by part of the territory becoming independent (such as the [[Ireland national football team (1882–1950)|Ireland national football team]], which ceased to exist as such after reaching a compromise with FIFA and recognising the [[Republic of Ireland national football team]] as representative of the independent part of [[Ireland]]). Other teams have disappeared by virtue of the states or territories they represented forming a new state by joining another entity or entities (examples include the teams representing [[Tanganyika national football team|Tanganyika]] and [[Malaya national football team|Malaya]], which merged with other former colonies to respectively form the Tanzania and [[Malaysia national football team|Malaysia]] football teams), or becoming part of an already existing state (as is the case with [[East Germany national football team|East Germany]], which joined West Germany to form the unified [[Germany national football team|Germany]] football team). In several cases the football records of dissolved teams are considered by FIFA as belonging to a successor entity (the [[Russia national football team|Russia]] national team, for example, carries over the records of the [[Soviet Union national football team|Soviet Union]] national team). Defunct teams are listed on this page for historical purposes.

Even if only members of FIFA and its affiliated confederations are taken into account, there are more national association football teams in the world than those of any other sport.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Brown|first1=Michael|title=Biggest Global Sports|url=http://biggestglobalsports.com/worlds-biggest-sports/4580873435|access-date=11 September 2015|archive-date=10 August 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150810010824/http://biggestglobalsports.com/worlds-biggest-sports/4580873435|url-status=live}}</ref>

== Members of FIFA affiliated confederations ==
{{MOS|section|[[MOS:NOSYMBOLS]]|date=April 2026}}
[[File:World Map FIFA.svg|thumb|400px|Map of the World with the six confederations:

{{legend|#ffb6c1|[[Asian Football Confederation|Asian Football Confederation (AFC)]]}}
{{legend|#deb887|[[Confederation of African Football|Confederation of African Football (CAF)]]}}
{{legend|#db7093|[[CONCACAF|Confederation of North, Central America and Caribbean Association Football (CONCACAF)]]}}
{{legend|#8fbc8f|[[CONMEBOL|South American Football Confederation (CONMEBOL)]]}}
{{legend|#ffd700|[[Oceania Football Confederation|Oceania Football Confederation (OFC)]]}}
{{legend|#4682b4|[[UEFA|Union of European Football Associations (UEFA)]]}}
]]

This section lists the current:

* 211 men's national football teams affiliated to FIFA, through their national football associations.
* 11 men's national football teams who have membership in one of FIFA's affiliated continental confederations, but are not members of FIFA.<ref>These are displayed in the main list in ''italics''.</ref>

FIFA members are eligible to enter the [[FIFA World Cup]] and matches between them are recognised as official international matches. Based on their match results over the previous four-year period, the [[FIFA Men's World Rankings]], published monthly by FIFA, compare the relative strengths of the national teams.

Some national teams that are members of a confederation but not FIFA members compete in confederation-level and subregional tournaments. These teams, however, are not allowed to participate in the World Cup.

The six confederations are:

* Asia – ''[[Asian Football Confederation]]'' (AFC){{efn|name=UAFA|group=note|Additionally, 22 nations in Africa and Asia belong to the [[Union of Arab Football Associations]] (UAFA) in addition to their respective regional confederations.}} 
* Africa – ''[[Confederation of African Football]]'' (CAF){{efn|name=UAFA|group=note}}
* North and Central America and the Caribbean – ''[[CONCACAF|Confederation of North, Central America and Caribbean Association Football]]'' (CONCACAF)
* South America – ''[[CONMEBOL|South American Football Confederation]]'' (CONMEBOL)
* Oceania – ''[[Oceania Football Confederation]]'' (OFC)
* Europe – ''[[UEFA|Union of European Football Associations]]'' (UEFA)

FIFA runs the [[FIFA World Cup|World Cup]] as a tournament for national teams to find the [[world champion]]. Each confederation also runs its own championship to find the best team from among its members:

*AFC – [[AFC Asian Cup]]
*CAF – [[Africa Cup of Nations]]
*CONCACAF – [[CONCACAF Gold Cup]]
*CONMEBOL – [[Copa América]]
*OFC – [[OFC Men's Nations Cup]]
*UEFA – [[UEFA European Football Championship]]

The [[Union of Arab Football Associations]] (UAFA) organises competitions between [[Arab League]] member nations.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.the-afc.com/media/afc-and-uafa-sign-memorandum-of-understanding-25263 | title=AFC and UAFA sign Memorandum of Understanding | date=1 May 2015 | publisher=AFC | access-date=5 December 2020 | archive-date=18 October 2021 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211018073008/https://www.the-afc.com/media/afc-and-uafa-sign-memorandum-of-understanding-25263 | url-status=live }}</ref> All 22 national governing bodies that form UAFA are also members of both FIFA and either the AFC or CAF. National teams from UAFA member countries are noted in the list below. The [[Arab Cup]] is the top championship tournament for national teams, organised historically by UAFA and by FIFA since 2021.

The [[Confederation of Independent Football Associations]] (ConIFA) is an organisation for teams representing unrecognised states, subnational regions, and stateless minorities, as well as teams from recognised states that have not managed to gain entry into FIFA. ConIFA is a successor to the [[Nouvelle Fédération-Board]] (N.F.-Board), which also organised tournaments for non-FIFA member teams. While none of the current ConIFA members are also members of FIFA, a few former members simultaneously held associate membership in one of the confederations affiliated with it. These teams are also noted in the list below.<ref name="auto">{{cite web |title=Members |url=http://www.conifa.org/en/members/ |publisher=CONIFA |access-date=15 June 2018 |archive-date=6 June 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230606234751/http://www.conifa.org/en/members/ |url-status=live }}</ref> The [[ConIFA World Football Cup]] is the top tournament for ConIFA member nations.

=== AFC ([[Asia]]) ===
Due to the geographical size of Asia, the AFC is subdivided into five sub-federations:

*[[West Asian Football Federation]] (WAFF) – represents countries at the [[Western Asia|western extremity of the continent]], except Israel.
*[[East Asian Football Federation]] (EAFF) – represents nations in East Asia, plus Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands.
*[[Central Asian Football Association]] (CAFA) – represents countries in Central Asia, comprising Afghanistan, Iran and most of [[Central Asia]], except Kazakhstan.
*[[South Asian Football Federation]] (SAFF) – represents countries in South Asia.
*[[ASEAN Football Federation]] (AFF) – represents countries in Southeast Asia, plus Australia.

{|
|-
| style="vertical-align:top; width:5%;"|
* {{fb|Afghanistan}}
* {{fb|Australia}}<sup>1¥</sup>
* {{fb|Bahrain}}<sup>2</sup>
* {{fb|Bangladesh}}
* {{fb|Bhutan}}
* {{fb|Brunei|name=Brunei Darussalam}}
* {{fb|Cambodia}}
* {{fb|China|name=China PR}}<sup>3</sup>
* {{fb|Chinese Taipei}}<sup>4</sup>
* {{fb|Guam}}
* {{fb|Hong Kong, China|name= Hong Kong, China}}<sup>5</sup>
* {{fb|India}}
| style="vertical-align:top; width:5%;"|
* {{fb|Indonesia}}
* {{fb|Iraq}}<sup>2¥</sup>
* {{fb|Iran|name=IR Iran}}<sup>6¥</sup>
* {{fb|Japan}}<sup>¥</sup>
* {{fb|Jordan}}<sup>2</sup>
* {{fb|North Korea|name=Korea DPR}}<sup>7</sup>
* {{fb|South Korea|name=Korea Republic}}<sup>8¥</sup>
* {{fb|Kuwait}}<sup>2¥</sup>
* {{fb|Kyrgyzstan|name=Kyrgyz Republic}}<sup>9</sup>
* {{fb|Laos}}
* {{fb|Lebanon}}<sup>2</sup>
* {{fb|Macau}}
| style="vertical-align:top; width:6%;"|
* {{fb|Malaysia}}
* {{fb|Maldives}}
* {{fb|Mongolia}}
* {{fb|Myanmar}}
* {{fb|Nepal}}
* ''{{fb|Northern Mariana Islands}}''<sup>10,11</sup>
* {{fb|Oman}}<sup>2</sup>
* {{fb|Pakistan}}
* {{fb|Palestine}}<sup>2</sup>
* {{fb|Philippines}}
* {{fb|Qatar}}<sup>2¥</sup>
* {{fb|Saudi Arabia}}<sup>2¥</sup>
| style="vertical-align:top; width:5%;"|
* {{fb|Singapore}}
* {{fb|Sri Lanka}}
* {{fb|Syria}}<sup>2</sup>
* {{fb|Tajikistan}}
* {{fb|Thailand}}
* {{fb|TLS|name=Timor-Leste}}
* {{fb|Turkmenistan}}
* {{fb|United Arab Emirates}}<sup>2</sup>
* {{fb|Uzbekistan}}
* {{fb|Vietnam}}
* {{fb|Yemen}}<sup>2</sup>
|} 
# National governing body was formerly a member of [[Oceania Football Confederation|OFC]] (1966–2006)
# National governing body is a member of [[Union of Arab Football Associations|UAFA]]
# Official name used by FIFA and AFC for [[China|People's Republic of China]] 
# Official name used by FIFA and AFC for [[Taiwan|Republic of China (Taiwan)]]; national governing body was a member of [[Oceania Football Confederation|OFC]] from 1975 to 1989
# Official name used by FIFA and AFC for [[Hong Kong]]
# Official name used by FIFA and AFC for [[Iran|Islamic Republic of Iran]]
# Official name used by FIFA for [[North Korea|Democratic People's Republic of Korea]]; official name used by AFC is DPR Korea
# Official name used by FIFA and AFC for [[South Korea|Republic of Korea]]
# Official name used by FIFA and AFC for [[Kyrgyzstan]]
# National governing body is a full member of AFC but not a FIFA member
# National governing body was formerly a member of [[Oceania Football Confederation|OFC]] (2005–2009)
:¥ AFC Asian Cup winner

=== CAF ([[Africa]]) ===
Due to the geographical size of Africa, CAF is divided into five regional federations:

*[[Council for East and Central Africa Football Associations]] (CECAFA) – represents nations generally regarded as forming the regions of East Africa and some nations of Central Africa.
*[[Council of Southern Africa Football Associations]] (COSAFA) – represents nations generally regarded as forming Southern Africa, as well as island states off the coast of Southern Africa.
*[[West African Football Union]] (WAFU/UFOA) – represents nations in West Africa.
*[[Union of North African Football Federations|Union of North African Federations]] (UNAF) – represents nations regarded as forming North Africa.
*[[Central African Football Federations' Union]]  (UNIFFAC) – represents some of the nations that form Central Africa.

{|
|-
| style="vertical-align:top; width:12%;"|
* {{fb|Algeria}}<sup>1₳</sup>
* {{fb|Angola}}
* {{fb|Benin}}
* {{fb|Botswana}}
* {{fb|Burkina Faso}}
* {{fb|Burundi}}
* {{fb|Cape Verde|name=Cabo Verde}}
* {{fb|Cameroon}}<sup>₳</sup>
* {{fb|Central African Republic}}
* {{fb|Chad}}
* {{fb|Comoros}}<sup>1</sup>
* {{fb|Congo}}<sup>₳</sup>
* {{fb|DR Congo|name=Congo DR}}<sup>2₳</sup>
* {{fb|Ivory Coast|name=Côte d'Ivoire}}<sup>₳</sup>
| style="vertical-align:top; width:10%;"|
* {{fb|Djibouti}}<sup>1</sup>
* {{fb|Egypt}}<sup>1₳</sup>
* {{fb|Equatorial Guinea}}
* {{fb|Eritrea}}
* {{fb|Eswatini}}
* {{fb|Ethiopia}}<sup>₳</sup>
* {{fb|Gabon}}
* {{fb|Gambia|name=The Gambia}}
* {{fb|Ghana}}<sup>₳</sup>
* {{fb|Guinea}}
* {{fb|Guinea-Bissau}}
* {{fb|Kenya}}
* {{fb|Lesotho}}
* {{fb|Liberia}}
| style="vertical-align:top; width:10%;"|
* {{fb|Libya}}<sup>1</sup>
* {{fb|Madagascar}}
* {{fb|Malawi}}
* {{fb|Mali}}
* {{fb|Mauritania}}<sup>1</sup>
* {{fb|Mauritius}}
* {{fb|Morocco}}<sup>1₳</sup>
* {{fb|Mozambique}}
* {{fb|Namibia}}
* {{fb|Niger}}
* {{fb|Nigeria}}<sup>₳</sup>
* ''{{fb|Réunion}}''<sup>3</sup>
* {{fb|Rwanda}}
* {{fb|São Tomé and Príncipe}}
| style="vertical-align:top; width:10%;"|
* {{fb|Senegal}}
* {{fb|Seychelles}}
* {{fb|Sierra Leone}}
* {{fb|Somalia}}<sup>1</sup>
* {{fb|South Africa}}<sup>₳</sup>
* {{fb|South Sudan}}
* {{fb|Sudan}}<sup>1₳</sup>
* {{fb|Tanzania}}
* {{fb|Togo}}
* {{fb|Tunisia}}<sup>1₳</sup>
* {{fb|Uganda}}
* {{fb|Zambia}}<sup>₳</sup>
* ''{{fb|Zanzibar}}''<sup>3,4,5</sup>
* {{fb|Zimbabwe}}
|}
# National governing body is a member of [[Union of Arab Football Associations|UAFA]]
# Official name used by FIFA for [[Democratic Republic of the Congo]]; official name used by CAF is DR Congo
# National governing body is an associate member of CAF but not a FIFA member
# National governing body was a full member of CAF briefly during 2017
# National governing body was previously a member of the [[N.F.-Board]] and [[ConIFA]]
:₳. African Cup of Nations winners

=== CONCACAF ([[North America]]) ===
The CONCACAF federation is divided into three regional federations that have responsibility for part of the region's geographical area:

* [[Caribbean Football Union]] (CFU) – represents all 27 nations in the [[Caribbean]], plus [[Bermuda]] and three nations in [[South America]].{{efn|group=note|Guyana and Suriname are independent countries, and French Guiana is an [[overseas department and region]] of France}}
* [[North American Football Union]] (NAFU) – represents the three countries in North America (not including Central America).
* [[Union Centroamericana de Fútbol]] (UNCAF) – represents the seven countries in Central America.

{|
|-
| style="vertical-align:top; width:8%;" |
* {{fb|Anguilla}}
* {{fb|Antigua and Barbuda}}
* {{fb|Aruba}}
* {{fb|Bahamas}}
* {{fb|Barbados}}
* {{fb|Belize}}
* {{fb|Bermuda}}
* ''{{fb|Bonaire}}''<sup>1</sup>
* {{fb|British Virgin Islands}}
* {{fb|Canada}}<sup>₵</sup>
* {{fb|Cayman Islands}}
| style="vertical-align:top; width:7%;" |
* {{fb|Costa Rica}}<sup>₵</sup>
* {{fb|Cuba}}
* {{fb|Curaçao}}
* {{fb|Dominica}}
* {{fb|Dominican Republic}}
* {{fb|El Salvador}}
* ''{{fb|French Guiana}}''<sup>1</sup>
* {{fb|Grenada}}
* ''{{fb|Guadeloupe}}''<sup>1</sup> 
* {{fb|Guatemala}}<sup>₵</sup>
* {{fb|Guyana}}
| style="vertical-align:top; width:10%;" |
* {{fb|Haiti}}<sup>₵</sup>
* {{fb|Honduras}}<sup>₵</sup>
* {{fb|Jamaica}}
* ''{{fb|Martinique}}''<sup>1</sup>
* {{fb|Mexico}}<sup>₵</sup>
* {{fb|Montserrat}}
* {{fb|Nicaragua}}
* {{fb|Panama}}
* {{fb|Puerto Rico}}
* {{fb|Saint Kitts and Nevis}}
* {{fb|Saint Lucia}}
| style="vertical-align:top; width:10%;" |
* ''{{fb|Saint Martin}}''<sup>1</sup>
* {{fb|Saint Vincent and the Grenadines}}
* ''{{fb|Sint Maarten}}''<sup>1</sup>
* {{fb|Suriname}}
* {{fb|Trinidad and Tobago}}
* {{fb|Turks and Caicos Islands}}
* {{fb|United States}}<sup>₵</sup>
* {{fb|United States Virgin Islands|name=US Virgin Islands}}
|}
#National governing body is a full member of CONCACAF but not a FIFA member
:₵. CONCACAF Championship / Gold Cup winners

=== CONMEBOL ([[South America]]) ===
{|
|-
| style="vertical-align:top; width:2%;"|
* {{fb|Argentina}}<sup>$</sup>
* {{fb|Bolivia}}<sup>$</sup>
* {{fb|Brazil}}<sup>$</sup>
* {{fb|Chile}}<sup>$</sup>
* {{fb|Colombia}}<sup>$</sup>
| style="vertical-align:top; width:5%;"|
* {{fb|Ecuador}}
* {{fb|Paraguay}}<sup>$</sup>
* {{fb|Peru}}<sup>$</sup>
* {{fb|Uruguay}}<sup>$</sup>
* {{fb|Venezuela}}
|-
|}

:$. South American Championship / Copa América winners

=== OFC ([[Oceania]]) ===
{|
|-
| style="vertical-align:top; width:2%;"|
* {{fb|American Samoa}}
* {{fb|Cook Islands}}
* {{fb|Fiji}}
* ''{{fb|Kiribati}}''<sup>1,2</sup>
* {{fb|New Caledonia}}
* {{fb|New Zealand}}<sup>3₴</sup>
* {{fb|Papua New Guinea}}
| style="vertical-align:top; width:5%;"|
* {{fb|Samoa}}
* {{fb|Solomon Islands}} 
* {{fb|Tahiti}}<sup>4₴</sup>
* {{fb|Tonga}}
* ''{{fb|Tuvalu}}''<sup>1,5</sup>
* {{fb|Vanuatu}}
|}

# National governing body is an associate member of the OFC but not a FIFA member
# National governing body was formerly a member of [[ConIFA]] (2016–unknown)
# National governing body was formerly a member of the [[Asian Football Confederation|AFC]] (1964–1966)
# Official name used by FIFA and the OFC for [[French Polynesia]]
# National governing body was formerly a member of [[ConIFA]] (2016–2020)
:₴. Oceania Cup / OFC Nations Cup winners

=== UEFA ([[Europe]]) ===
{|
|-
| style="vertical-align:top; width:5%;"|
* {{fb|Albania}}
* {{fb|Andorra}}
* {{fb|Armenia}}
* {{fb|Austria}}
* {{fb|Azerbaijan}}
* {{fb|Belarus}}
* {{fb|Belgium}}
* {{fb|Bosnia and Herzegovina}}
* {{fb|Bulgaria}}
* {{fb|Croatia}}
* {{fb|Cyprus}}
* {{fb|Czech Republic|name=Czechia}}
* {{fb|Denmark}}<sup>€</sup>
* {{fb|England}}
| style="vertical-align:top; width:5%;"|
* {{fb|Estonia}}
* {{fb|Faroe Islands}}
* {{fb|Finland}}
* {{fb|France}}<sup>€</sup>
* {{fb|Georgia}}
* {{fb|Germany}}<sup>€</sup>
* {{fb|Gibraltar}}
* {{fb|Greece}}<sup>€</sup>
* {{fb|Hungary}}
* {{fb|Iceland}}
* {{fb|Israel}}<sup>1</sup>
* {{fb|Italy}}<sup>€</sup>
* {{fb|Kazakhstan}}<sup>2</sup>
* {{fb|Kosovo}}
| style="vertical-align:top; width:5%;"|
* {{fb|Latvia}}
* {{fb|Liechtenstein}}
* {{fb|Lithuania}}
* {{fb|Luxembourg}}
* {{fb|Malta}}
* {{fb|Moldova}}
* {{fb|Montenegro}}
* {{fb|Netherlands}}<sup>€</sup>
* {{fb|Northern Ireland}}
* {{fb|North Macedonia}}
* {{fb|Norway}}
* {{fb|Poland}}
* {{fb|Portugal}}<sup>€</sup>
* {{fb|Republic of Ireland}}
| style="vertical-align:top; width:5%;"|
* {{fb|Romania}}
* {{fb|Russia}}<sup>3€</sup>
* {{fb|San Marino}}
* {{fb|Scotland}}
* {{fb|Serbia}}
* {{fb|Slovakia}}
* {{fb|Slovenia}}
* {{fb|Spain}}<sup>€</sup>
* {{fb|Sweden}}
* {{fb|Switzerland}}
* {{fb|Türkiye|name=Türkiye}}
* {{fb|Ukraine}}
* {{fb|Wales}}
|}
# National governing body was formerly a member of [[Asian Football Confederation|AFC]] (1954–1974); joined UEFA in 1994
# National governing body was formerly a member of [[Asian Football Confederation|AFC]] (1993–2002)
# National team currently suspended from participation in FIFA and UEFA competitions in response to Russia's [[Russian invasion of Ukraine|2022 invasion of Ukraine]]<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.fifa.com/tournaments/mens/worldcup/qatar2022/media-releases/fifa-uefa-suspend-russian-clubs-and-national-teams-from-all-competitions | title=FIFA/UEFA suspend Russian clubs and national teams from all competitions | access-date=2 July 2022 | archive-date=28 February 2022 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220228173247/https://www.fifa.com/tournaments/mens/worldcup/qatar2022/media-releases/fifa-uefa-suspend-russian-clubs-and-national-teams-from-all-competitions | url-status=live }}</ref>
:€. UEFA European Championship winners

== National teams not affiliated to FIFA confederations ==
The national football teams included in this section are not members of FIFA, or of any of its affiliated continental confederations. The teams are not eligible to enter the FIFA World Cup or any continental confederation championships. FIFA's statutes do not allow member teams to compete against these sides without FIFA's prior permission.<ref>{{cite web|title=FIFA Statutes: July 2012 Edition|url=https://www.fifa.com/mm/document/affederation/generic/01/66/54/21/fifastatutes2012e.pdf|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120802075510/http://www.fifa.com/mm/document/affederation/generic/01/66/54/21/fifastatutes2012e.pdf|archive-date=2 August 2012|publisher=FIFA|access-date=7 January 2013|at=Article 83}}</ref> Several national associations for teams included in this section are members of [[ConIFA]]; these are indicated in the lists below.

This section lists:
* 6 teams representing [[sovereign state]]s which are member states of the [[United Nations]] or non-member observer states with the [[United Nations General Assembly]].
* 7 teams representing states which are not members of the United Nations.

===Unaffiliated United Nations states===
Five UN members and one UN General Assembly observer state are not members of FIFA or any continental federation, but have fielded national association-organised teams in matches outside the auspices of FIFA. The national teams of these six states are listed below.

<!--Please do not add/remove teams, edit team's names, or edit/remove footnotes without discussing on talk page first. A lot of effort has been made to maintain consistency across the page. Team names should respect the naming on official confederation websites (such as the Pacific Games Council or ConIFA). -->
* {{fb|FSM|name=Federated States of Micronesia}}
* {{fb|MHL}}
* {{fb|MON}}<sup>1</sup>
* {{fb|Palau}}<sup>2</sup>
* {{fb|UK}}<sup>3</sup>
* {{fb|VAT}}
# National governing body was previously a member of the [[N.F.-Board]] and [[Confederation of Independent Football Associations|ConIFA]].
# National governing body has been an associate member of the OFC in the past, but does not currently appear to be part of the confederation.<ref name="auto1">{{cite web|url=http://nonfifafootball.blogspot.com/2013/04/interview-6-april-2013-palau-football.html|title=World Cups and Beyond: Interview #6 (April 2013): Palau Football {{sic|Asss|ociation|nolink=y}} president Charles Mitchell.|date=26 April 2013|access-date=18 September 2019|archive-date=7 February 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200207111626/http://nonfifafootball.blogspot.com/2013/04/interview-6-april-2013-palau-football.html|url-status=live}}</ref> 
# The [[United Kingdom]] (UK) is not a member of FIFA or UEFA in its own right, being represented instead by the teams of its four constituent nations (England, Northern Ireland, Scotland, and Wales). However, an exhibition UK team has played a small number of friendly matches.<ref>{{cite web |title=England Player Honours - International Representative Teams |url=http://www.englandfootballonline.com/TeamHons/HonsRepTms.html |publisher=England Football Online |access-date=29 September 2021 |archive-date=30 August 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210830131316/http://www.englandfootballonline.com/TeamHons/HonsRepTms.html |url-status=live }}</ref> The UK has also been represented in the [[Great Britain men's Olympic football team|Summer Olympic]] and [[Football at the Summer Universiade|Summer Universiade]] football competitions.

<!--Please do not add flags for Nauru. This country has no official football team and therefore no flag should be included. See the following guideline: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Icons#Inappropriate_use_2 -->
[[Nauru]] is the only UN member state to have never fielded a national association-organised football team.<ref>{{cite web |title=Nauru 2014 |website=[[RSSSF]] |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesn/nauru2014.html |access-date=13 February 2019 |archive-date=5 August 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220805203412/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesn/nauru2014.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

=== Unaffiliated non-UN states ===
Three [[List of states with limited recognition|states with limited international recognition]] and no UN membership are members of both FIFA and an affiliated confederation: the [[Taiwan|Republic of China]] (as Chinese Taipei), [[Kosovo]], and [[Palestine]]. The [[Cook Islands]] is an [[associated state]] with no UN membership, but it is a member of both FIFA and the OFC. The national teams representing these states are all listed above.

A further seven associated, de facto, or partially recognised states with no UN membership have fielded football teams in non-FIFA football tournaments or FIFA-unsanctioned friendly matches.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://vestnikkavkaza.net/news/sport/34442.html |title=Abkhazia founds national football team |publisher=Vestnik Kavkaza |date=3 December 2012 |access-date=7 July 2016 |archive-date=1 June 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160601142952/http://vestnikkavkaza.net/news/sport/34442.html |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://worldfootballcup.org/team/nagorno-karabakh-fa/ |title=Nagorno Karabakh FA {{pipe}} ConIFA |access-date=10 June 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140607100019/http://worldfootballcup.org/team/nagorno-karabakh-fa/ |archive-date=7 June 2014 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://conifaofficial.wordpress.com/somaliland/|title=Somaliland|date=31 July 2015|work=wordpress.com|access-date=4 July 2016|archive-date=5 March 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305011319/https://conifaofficial.wordpress.com/somaliland/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://nonfifafootball.blogspot.co.uk/2013/10/south-ossetia-make-international-bow-in.html|title=Non-FIFA Football Updates: South Ossetia make international bow in Abkhazia loss|work=nonfifafootball.blogspot.co.uk|date=10 October 2013|access-date=4 July 2016|archive-date=7 March 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160307203612/http://nonfifafootball.blogspot.co.uk/2013/10/south-ossetia-make-international-bow-in.html|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://ffpmr.md/turniry/|title=Tournaments (Russian)|date=27 March 2018|website=www.ffpmr.md/|access-date=11 April 2019|archive-date=25 November 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221125145605/https://ffpmr.md/turniry/|url-status=live}}</ref> None of these states, however, are currently members of FIFA or any of its affiliated continental confederations. The teams representing these states are listed below.

<!--Please do not add/remove teams, edit team's names, or edit/remove footnotes without discussing on talk page first. A lot of effort has been made to maintain consistency across the page. Team names should respect the naming on official confederation websites (including but not limited to FIFA-affiliated confederations and ConIFA). -->
* {{fb|Abkhazia}}<sup>1</sup>
<!--Even if the state of Artsakh has stopped existing, we have no confirmation the same has happened to the football team. This entry should be kept for at least as long as Artsakh remains listed as ConIFA member. It can be moved to former national teams afterwards-->
* {{fb|Niue}}<sup>2</sup>
* {{fb|Northern Cyprus}}<sup>1,3</sup>
* {{fb|Somaliland}}<sup>1</sup>
* {{fb|South Ossetia}}<sup>1</sup>
* {{fb|Transnistria}}<sup>4</sup>
* {{flagdeco|Western Sahara}} [[Western Sahara national football team|Western Sahara]]<sup>1,3</sup>

# National governing body is currently a member of [[ConIFA]].
# National governing body was previously an associate member of the OFC (membership revoked in March 2021).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.rnz.co.nz/international/pacific-news/437764/niue-removed-as-associate-member-of-oceania-football |title=Niue removed as associate member of Oceania Football |author=<!--Not stated--> |date=6 March 2021 |website=[[RNZ]] |access-date=10 July 2021 |quote= |archive-date=8 October 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221008054517/https://www.rnz.co.nz/international/pacific-news/437764/niue-removed-as-associate-member-of-oceania-football |url-status=live }}</ref>
# National governing body was previously a member of the [[N.F.-Board]].
# National governing body was previously a member of [[ConIFA]] (2015-unknown).

===Others===
{{See also|Non-FIFA international football}}
Many other teams compete in representative football outside of FIFA's oversight, and some of these claim 'national' status. Historically FIFA did not tightly define "country" and as such twenty-three current FIFA members represent subnational and dependent territories, as well as three representing states [[List of states with limited recognition|with limited international recognition]].{{efn|group=note|The FIFA-affiliated football teams that belong to non-UN members are:
{{col-begin|width=65%}}
{{col-3}} 
''' 4 [[List of states with limited recognition|states with limited international recognition]]'''
*{{fb|Chinese Taipei}}
*{{fb|Cook Islands}}
*{{fb|Kosovo}}
*{{fb|Palestine}} (also a UN observer state)
''' 4 [[Countries of the United Kingdom|constituent countries]] of the United Kingdom'''
*{{fb|England}}
*{{fb|Northern Ireland}}
*{{fb|Scotland}}
*{{fb|Wales}}
{{col-3}}
''' 7 [[British Overseas Territories]]'''
*{{fb|Anguilla}}
*{{fb|Bermuda}}
*{{fb|British Virgin Islands}}
*{{fb|Cayman Islands}}
*{{fb|Gibraltar}}
*{{fb|Montserrat}}
*{{fb|Turks and Caicos Islands}}
''' 2 constituent countries of the [[Kingdom of the Netherlands]]'''
*{{fb|Aruba}}
*{{fb|Curaçao}}
''' 1 constituent country of the [[Danish Realm|Kingdom of Denmark]]'''
*{{fb|Faroe Islands}}
{{col-3}}
''' 4 [[Insular area|unincorporated territories]] of the United States'''
*{{fb|American Samoa}}
*{{fb|Guam}}
*{{fb|Puerto Rico}}
*{{fb|United States Virgin Islands}}
''' 2 [[Overseas collectivity|overseas collectivities]] of France'''
*{{fb|New Caledonia}}
*{{fb|Tahiti}}
''' 2 [[Special administrative regions of China|special administrative regions]] of China'''
*{{fb|Hong Kong, China|name=Hong Kong, China}}
*{{fb|Macau}}
{{col-end}}
}} A further nine overseas, dependent, and autonomous territories with close ties to a sovereign state do not have membership in FIFA, but are members of one of its affiliated confederations (either in a full or associate capacity). 
In 2016 however, FIFA made changes to its statutes to define 'country' as "an independent state recognized by the international community",<ref>{{cite web |title=FIFA Statutes |url=http://resources.fifa.com/mm/document/affederation/generic/02/78/29/07/fifastatutsweben_neutral.pdf |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160509164416/http://resources.fifa.com/mm/Document/AFFederation/Generic/02/78/29/07/FIFAStatutswebEN_Neutral.pdf |archive-date=9 May 2016 |publisher=FIFA |access-date=12 April 2019 |page=4}}</ref> which has made membership of FIFA harder for aspirant national teams. Nonetheless, several organisations exist to encourage, facilitate or promote representative football outside of FIFA, and many of their members claim the status of national football teams:
* [[Confederation of Independent Football Associations]] (CONIFA)
* [[International Island Games Association]] (IIGA)
* [[Union of Arab Football Associations]] (UAFA)
* [[Football at the Indian Ocean Island Games|Indian Ocean Island Games]] 
* [[Football at the Pacific Games|Pacific Games]]

== Teams representing former states or territories ==
These national teams no longer exist due to the dissolution of the nation or territory that they represented. Only teams that held FIFA membership at some point, or those that represented [[states with limited recognition]] and have been members of the N.F.-Board or ConIFA, are included on the table. 

{| class="wikitable"
! width=245| Preceding team
! width=100| Successor team<br />(inherited position/results)
! width=200| Other successor team(s)
! Notes
|-
|  {{fb|Czechoslovakia}}<br /><small>(''Representation of Czechs and Slovaks'' in 1993)</small>
| colspan="2" style="text-align:center;"|  {{fb|Czech Republic}}<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Czechia |url=https://inside.fifa.com/associations/CZE |access-date=2025-08-15 |website=fifa.com |language=en}}</ref><br>{{fb|Slovakia}}<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Slovakia |url=https://inside.fifa.com/associations/SVK |access-date=2025-08-15 |website=fifa.com |language=en}}</ref>
|  Represented [[Czechoslovakia]] until its dissolution into the Czech Republic and Slovakia in 1993.<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" /> Jointly represented both nations during the remainder of their [[1994 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA – Group 4)|1994 World Cup qualifying]] games.<ref>{{cite web|title=World Cup Ends on Belgian Note|url=http://www.praguepost.com/archivescontent/12198-world-cup-ends-on-belgian-note.html|work=The Prague Post|access-date=18 July 2013|archive-date=12 November 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231112211321/https://www.praguepost.com/|url-status=live}}</ref>
|-
|  {{fb|Saar}}
|  colspan="2" | ''Joined the [[German Football Association]]; now part of the [[Germany national football team]]. Historical results and achievements listed separately.''
|  Represented the [[Saar (protectorate)|Saarland Protectorate]] from 1950 to 1956 before its union with the Federal Republic of Germany.<ref>{{cite web|title=Saarland 1950–1955|url=http://www.wsc.co.uk/the-archive/923-Europe/4367-saarland-1950-1955|publisher=When Saturday Comes|access-date=18 July 2013|archive-date=21 March 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140321044406/http://www.wsc.co.uk/the-archive/923-Europe/4367-saarland-1950-1955|url-status=live}}</ref> 
|-
|  {{fb|East Germany}}<br /><small>(officially German Democratic Republic)</small>
| colspan="2" | ''Joined the German Football Association; dissolved in November 1990. Historical results and achievements listed separately.''
|  Represented [[East Germany]] between 1952 and 1990, before [[German reunification|reunification]] with [[West Germany]].<ref name="East and West">{{cite web |title=Germany: When East and West became one |url=https://www.fifa.com/worldfootball/news/newsid=1339057.html |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101125092332/http://www.fifa.com/worldfootball/news/newsid=1339057.html |archive-date=25 November 2010 |access-date=18 July 2013 |publisher=FIFA}}</ref>
|-
|  {{fb|Ireland}}
|  {{fb|Northern Ireland}}
|  {{fb|Republic of Ireland}}
|  Represented Ireland from 1882. From 1922, when the [[Irish Free State]] (later [[Republic of Ireland]]) left the United Kingdom, until 1953, it continued to pick players from across [[Ireland|the Island of Ireland]], before becoming restricted to players solely from [[Northern Ireland]] under pressure from FIFA.<ref>{{cite book | title=Football Association of Ireland: 75 years | first=Peter | last=Byrne |year=1996 | location=[[Dublin]] | publisher=Sportsworld | isbn = 1-900110-06-7 | page=68}}</ref>
|-
|  {{fb|Malaya}}
|  colspan="2" style="text-align:center;"| {{fb|Malaysia}}
|  Represented the [[Federation of Malaya]] from 1953 until its union with [[Crown Colony of Sarawak|Sarawak]], [[Crown Colony of North Borneo|North Borneo]] and [[Singapore]] to form [[Malaysia]] in 1963. Singapore, which gained independence in 1965, retained its preexisting national team.
|-
| {{fb|Tanganyika (1961–1964)}} 
| colspan="2" style="text-align:center;"| {{fb|Tanzania}} 
| Represented [[Tanganyika (1961–1964)|Tanganyika]] until its union with [[Zanzibar]] as Tanzania in 1964. [[Zanzibar national football team|Zanzibar]] is an associate member of [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] and so is not a member of FIFA.
|-
|  {{fb|South Vietnam}}
|  colspan="2" style="text-align:center;"| {{fb|Vietnam}}
|  Represented [[South Vietnam]] from 1949 until 1975. A [[North Vietnam national football team|separate team]] representing North Vietnam never received FIFA or AFC membership. On the [[Reunification Day|reunification of Vietnam]], both the North and South teams ceased to exist, with a unified [[Vietnam national football team]] taking the South's place in both FIFA and the AFC.
|-
|  {{fb|North Yemen}}<br /><small>(officially Yemen Arab Republic)</small>
|  colspan="2" style="text-align:center;"|  {{fb|Yemen}}
|  Represented [[Yemen Arab Republic|North Yemen]] from 1965 until its union with [[South Yemen]] in 1990.
|-
|  {{fb|South Yemen}}<br /><small>(officially People's Democratic Republic of Yemen)</small>
|  colspan="2" | ''Joined the [[Yemen Football Association]]; now part of the [[Yemen national football team]]. Historical results and achievements listed separately.''
|  Represented [[South Yemen]] from 1965 until its union with [[Yemen Arab Republic|North Yemen]] in 1990.
|-
|  {{fb|United Arab Republic}}
|  {{fb|Egypt}}
|  {{fb|Syria|revolution}}
|  Represented the [[United Arab Republic]] from 1958 to 1961 until the secession of Syria. Was considered a continuation of the previous [[Egypt national football team]], which became its successor team. The team continued to be known as the United Arab Republic until 1970.
|-
|  {{fb|Mandatory Palestine}}
|  {{fb|Israel}}
|  {{fb|Palestine}}
|  Represented the [[Mandatory Palestine|British Mandate of Palestine]] from 1934 to 1940. The team ceased to exist after the creation of the [[State of Israel]] in 1948, with FIFA recognizing the [[Israel national football team]] as the successor to the Mandatory Palestine football team. A [[Palestine national football team|separate team]] representing the [[Palestinian Territories]] was created in 1953 and accepted into FIFA in 1998.
|-
|  {{fb|Russian Empire|name=Russian Empire}}
|  colspan="2" style="text-align:center;"| {{fb|URS|1992}}
|   Represented [[Russian Empire]] from 1912 to 1923 until its transition into [[Soviet Union]].
|-
|  {{fb|URS}}<br /><small>(officially Union of Soviet Socialist Republics)</small>
|  {{fb|CIS|1992}}
|  {{fb|EST}}<br />{{fb|LVA}}<br />{{fb|LTU}}
|   Represented the [[Soviet Union]] from 1940 until its dissolution in 1991. This was considered a continuation of the team that had previously represented the [[Russian Empire]]. Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania had all had active football teams prior to their [[Occupation of the Baltic states|incorporation into the Soviet Union in 1940]], and did not participate in the CIS team.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Furmanov |first1=Vadim |title=The National Team Without a Nation: The Story of the CIS at Euro 92 |date=5 January 2018 |url=http://www.futbolgrad.com/national-team-without-nation-cis/ |publisher=Futbolgrad |access-date=28 June 2022 |archive-date=6 July 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220706020851/https://www.futbolgrad.com/national-team-without-nation-cis/ |url-status=live }}</ref>
|-
|  {{fb|CIS}} 
|  {{fb|RUS}}
|  {{fb|ARM}}<br />{{fb|AZE}}<br />{{fb|BLR|1991}}<br />{{fb|GEO|1990}}<br />{{fb|KAZ}}<br />{{fb|KGZ}}<br />{{fb|MDA}}<br /> {{fb|TJK}}<br />{{fb|TKM}}<br />{{fb|UKR}}<br />{{fb|UZB}}
|  Represented the [[Commonwealth of Independent States]] from January to June 1992; until the end of the [[UEFA Euro 1992]] tournament. 
|-
|  {{fb|SFR Yugoslavia}}
|  {{Fb|FR Yugoslavia}}
|  {{fb|BIH|1992}}<br />{{fb|CRO}}<br />{{fb|MKD|name=North Macedonia}}<br />{{fb|SVN}}
|  Represented [[Yugoslavia]] between 1920 and 1992, before the dissolution of the [[Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia]] into [[Bosnia and Herzegovina]], [[Croatia]], [[Federal Republic of Yugoslavia]], [[North Macedonia]] and [[Slovenia]]
|-
|  {{fb|SCG}}
|  {{fb|SRB}}
|  {{fb|MNE|1992}}<br />{{fb|KOS|2008}}
|  Represented the [[Federal Republic of Yugoslavia]], known as [[Serbia and Montenegro]] after 2003, from 1992 until its dissolution into [[Serbia]] and [[Montenegro]] in 2006; the unified team still played the [[2006 FIFA World Cup]], because the dissolution process was only concluded one week before the start of the tournament with Montenegro's declaration of independence, following the referendum in May of that year. [[Kosovo]] [[2008 Kosovo declaration of independence|declared independence]] from Serbia in 2008, and its national team was accepted into UEFA and FIFA in 2016.
|-
| {{fb|ANT}}
| {{fb|CUW}}
| {{fb|ARU}}<br/>{{fb|Bonaire}}<br/>{{fb|Sint Maarten}}<br/>{{fb|Saba}}<br/>{{fb|Sint Eustatius}}
| [[Aruba]] became a separate autonomous territory in 1986 and was accepted into FIFA in 1988. The former team represented the [[Netherlands Antilles]] until the [[dissolution of the Netherlands Antilles|dissolution of the country]] in 2010. Formerly known as "Curaçao", this name was restored in March 2011 when the new constituent country of [[Curaçao]] was given the Netherlands Antilles' place in FIFA and CONCACAF. The teams representing the former Netherlands Antilles territories of [[Bonaire]] and [[Sint Maarten]] are full members of CONCACAF but not of FIFA. Two other former Netherlands Antilles territories ([[Saba national soccer team|Saba]] and [[Sint Eustatius national football team|Sint Eustatius]]) have fielded national teams in unofficial friendly matches in the past, but neither has membership of FIFA or a continental federation.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesi/iisabastatia.html|title=Inter Island Matches Saba/Sint Eustatius|website=[[RSSSF]]|access-date=2 February 2023|archive-date=26 December 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221226203043/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesi/iisabastatia.html|url-status=live}}</ref>
|}

===New names===

In addition to the above, other teams have been renamed:

*{{fb|Belgian Congo}} → {{fb|COD|1960|name=Congo-Léopoldville}} in 1960 → {{fb|COD|1963|name=Congo-Kinshasa}} in 1963 → {{fb|ZAI}} in 1971 → {{fb|COD|1997}} in 1997
*{{flagicon|GBR}} [[Gambia national football team|British Gambia]] → {{fb|Gambia}} in 1965
*{{fb|British Guiana}} → {{fb|GUY}} in 1966
*{{fb|British Honduras}} → {{fb|Belize}} in 1981
*{{flagicon|GBR}} [[Mauritius national football team|British Mauritius]] → {{fb|Mauritius}} in 1968
*{{flagicon|GBR}} [[Somalia national football team|British Somaliland]] → {{fb|Somalia}} in 1960
*{{fb|Burma|1948}} → {{fb|MYA|1974}} in 1989
*{{fb|CAM}} → {{fb|CAM|1970|name=Khmer Republic}} in 1970 → {{fb|CAM|1975|name=Kampuchea}} in 1975 → {{fb|CAM}} in 1979
*{{fb|Ceylon}} → {{fb|SRI}} in 1972
*{{fb|Territory of Curaçao}} → {{fb|ANT}} in 1958 until 2010
*{{fb|Czechoslovakia}} → {{fb|TCH|name=Representation of Czechs and Slovaks}} in 1993
*{{fb|CZE|name=Czech Republic}} → {{fb|CZE|name=Czechia}} in 2022
*{{fb|Dahomey}} → {{fb|BEN|1975}} in 1975
*{{fb|Dutch East Indies}} → {{fb|IDN}} in 1945
*{{Fb|EGY|variant=1922}} → {{fb|EGY|variant=1958|name=United Arab Republic}} in 1958 → {{fb|Egypt|1972}} in 1971
*{{fb|SCG|name=FR Yugoslavia}} → {{fb|SCG}} in 2003
*{{fb|North Macedonia|name=FYR Macedonia}} → {{fb|North Macedonia}} in 2019
*{{fb|French Somaliland}} → {{fb|DJI}} in 1977
*{{fb|Togo|1957|name=French Togoland}} → {{fb|TOG}} in 1960
*{{Fb|GER|variant=1935}} → {{Fb|FRG|name=Germany FR}} in 1945 → {{Fb|GER}} in 1990
*{{fb|Gold Coast}} → {{fb|GHA}} in 1957
*{{fb|Irish Free State}} → {{fb|Republic of Ireland|name=Ireland}} in 1936 → {{fb|Republic of Ireland}} in 1954
*{{fb|CIV|name=Ivory Coast}} → {{fb|CIV|name=Côte d'Ivoire}} in 1983
*{{fb|Kingdom of Yugoslavia|name=Kingdom of Serbs, Croats, and Slovenes}} → {{fb|Kingdom of Yugoslavia}} in 1929
*{{flagicon|FRA}} [[Madagascar national football team|Madagascar]] → {{fb|MAD|name=Malagasy Republic}} in 1958 → {{fb|MAD}} in 1975
*{{fb|Mandatory Palestine}} → {{Fb|ISR}} in 1948
*{{flagicon|FRA}} [[Congo national football team|Middle Congo]] → {{fb|CGO|name=Congo-Brazzaville}} in 1960 → {{fb|CGO|1970|name=PR Congo}} in 1970 → {{fb|CGO}} in 1992
*{{fb|New Hebrides}} → {{fb|VAN}} in 1980
*{{fb|Northern Rhodesia}} → {{fb|ZAM}} in 1964
*{{fb|Nyasaland}} → {{fb|MWI|1964}} in 1966
*{{flagicon|POR}} [[Mozambique national football team|Portuguese East Africa]] → {{fb|Mozambique|1975}} in 1975
*{{flagicon|POR}} [[Guinea-Bissau national football team|Portuguese Guinea]] → {{fb|GNB}} in 1975
*{{flagicon|POR}} [[Angola national football team|Portuguese West Africa]] → {{fb|Angola}} in 1975
*{{Fb|Republic of China (1912–1949)|variant=1928}} → {{Fb|Taiwan}} in 1954 → {{Fb|TPE|variant=football-old}} in 1980
*{{fb|Southern Rhodesia}} → {{fb|Rhodesia}} in 1964 → {{fb|ZIM}} in 1980
*{{flagicon|NED}} [[Suriname national football team|Surinam]] → {{fb|NGY}} in 1954
*{{fb|SWZ|name=Swaziland}} → {{fb|SWZ|name=Eswatini}} in 2018
*{{fb|TUR|name=Turkey}} → {{fb|TUR|name=Türkiye}} in 2022
*{{fb|Upper Volta}} → {{fb|BFA}} in 1984
*{{Fb|State of Vietnam|name=Vietnam}} → {{Fb|South Vietnam}} in 1955
*{{fb|SAM|name=Western Samoa}} → {{fb|SAM}} in 1997

==Notes==
{{reflist|group=note}}

== See also ==
{{portal|Association football}}
*[[Geography of association football]]
*[[List of FIFA country codes]]
*[[List of women's national association football teams]]
*[[:Category:National and official selection-teams not affiliated to FIFA]]
*[[List of association football competitions]]

==References==
{{Reflist}}

<!-- ==See also==
* [[List of FIFA members by the year of their membership]] --><!-- link is moved to draft space -->

== External links ==
* {{cite web |url=https://www.fifa.com/associations/index.html |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120512233944/http://www.fifa.com/associations/index.html |archive-date=12 May 2012 |title=List of FIFA Associations |publisher=FIFA}}

{{International football}}
{{National football teams}}
{{Non-FIFA teams}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:List Of Men's National Association Football Teams}}
[[Category:Lists of association football national teams| ]]
[[Category:Men's national association football teams| ]]
